title: thymus zygis var. baeticus
intro: Thymus zygis var. baeticus, also known as Spanish Thyme, is a plant species in the Lamiaceae family, native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its antiseptic and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The medicinal properties of Spanish Thyme include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and boost the immune system, providing health benefits such as relieving coughs, colds, and sore throats. Spanish Thyme can be prepared as an herbal tea, infused oil, or tincture, making it a versatile and natural remedy for various health concerns.
